# Angular 20 Frontend Plan for quote-rest-api

## Checklist
- [ ] Choose frontend location and API base URL strategy
- [ ] Scaffold Angular 20 app and configure environments
- [ ] Define models and API services
- [ ] Build routes and UI for authors and quotes
- [ ] Add state caching, tests, and deployment config

## Detailed Plan

### 1) Project Setup
- Decide location: `frontend/` inside this repo vs separate repo.
- Scaffold Angular 20 with standalone components and modern control flow.
- Set `environment.ts` and `environment.prod.ts` with `apiBaseUrl`.
- Optional: configure a local dev proxy to avoid CORS in development.

### 2) API Discovery
- Use `references/ALL_ENDPOINTS_REFERENCE.md` to confirm endpoints.
- Expected endpoints:
  - `GET /api/v1/authors`
  - `GET /api/v1/authors/{id}/quotes`
- Verify response shapes and any pagination or sorting options.

### 3) Data Modeling
- Define `Author` and `Quote` interfaces.
- Add a generic API response wrapper if backend uses one.

### 4) Services
- `AuthorService`: fetch authors, apply A–Z sorting client-side if not server-sorted.
- `QuoteService`: fetch quotes by author id.
- Centralize error handling and loading state.

### 5) Routing & UI
- Routes:
  - `/authors` (default) -> Authors list
  - `/authors/:id` -> Author detail with quotes
- Components:
  - `AuthorsListComponent`: display authors A–Z; click to view detail.
  - `AuthorDetailComponent`: show author name and quotes list.
  - Shared UI: loading indicator, error banner, empty-state.

### 6) State & Caching
- Use lightweight signals or service-level store to cache authors and quotes.
- Avoid NgRx unless scope grows.

### 7) Tests
- Unit tests for services and components.
- Integration test for list + detail flow if time permits.

### 8) Build & Deployment
- Set `apiBaseUrl` via envs.
- Confirm CORS on backend or use dev proxy.
- Decide deployment: static hosting, Docker, or served with backend.
